key_insight: Machine learning potentials enabled rapid screening of graphene-supported nitrogen-coordinated dual-atom catalysts, revealing that intermediate coordination environments (2N–4N) most often produce near-optimal HER binding energetics.
engineering_relevance: This provides a scalable route to identify low-cost, potentially synthesizable HER electrocatalysts with noble-metal-like performance, reducing reliance on exhaustive DFT searches.
method: Built an MLP benchmarked against DFT for TM2@Nx-Gr dual-atom catalysts
Screened 23 transition metals across 20 nitrogen coordination motifs
Ranked candidates by Gibbs binding free energy (ΔGH*) and electronic character
evidence_signal: The model achieved a mean absolute error of 80 meV for Gibbs binding free energies relative to DFT while cutting computational cost by orders of magnitude.
watch_item: MLP-based screening still depends on the quality and coverage of the DFT training/benchmark set, so extrapolation to unexplored chemistries may require additional validation.
paper_title: Accelerated Discovery of Nitrogen-Coordinated Dual-Atom Hydrogen Evolution Reaction Electrocatalysts via Machine Learning Potentials
